title: alchemilla hoppeana var. grossidens
intro: Alchemilla hoppeana var. grossidens, also known as a type of alpine lady's mantle, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and tannins. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The herbal preparations of Alchemilla hoppeana var. grossidens include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its health benefits.
